ucs.title: [LLM] BlackByte BYOVD: RTCore64.sys (MSI Afterburner) loaded to disable EDR before encryption
ucs.description: Hunts BlackByte's signature 'Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver' chain where the operator drops the legitimate-but-vulnerable RTCore64.sys (CVE-2019-16098, MSI Afterburner) and/or DBUtil_2_3.sys to obtain kernel R/W and unhook ~1,000 EDR/AV callbacks immediately prior to ransomware deployment. This is a highly characteristic step documented by Sophos and the FBI/CISA #StopRansomware advisory (AA22-321A) for BlackByte 2.0 / Hecamede.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name=sc.exe OR Processes.process_name=sc) (Processes.process="*create*" OR Processes.process="*config*") (Processes.process="*RTCore64*" OR Processes.process="*DBUtil_2_3*" OR Processes.process="*BlackByte*" OR Processes.process="*type= kernel*") by host Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.user 
| append [ | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name="RTCore64.sys" OR Filesystem.file_name="DBUtil_2_3.sys") AND NOT Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\Program Files*\\MSI Afterburner*","*\\Dell*") by host Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.process_name ]
| append [ | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where Registry.registry_path="*\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\RTCore64*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\Services\\BlackByte*" by host Registry.registry_path Registry.registry_value_name Registry.process_name ]
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let suspiciousDriverFiles = dynamic(["RTCore64.sys","DBUtil_2_3.sys","gdrv.sys"]);
let driverDrop =
  DeviceFileEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
  | where FileName in~ (suspiciousDriverFiles)
  | where not(FolderPath has_any (@"\Program Files\MSI Afterburner", @"\Program Files (x86)\MSI Afterburner", @"\Dell\"))
  | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, FolderPath, FileName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, SHA256;
let svcCreate =
  DeviceProcessEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
  | where FileName =~ "sc.exe"
  | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("RTCore64","DBUtil_2_3","BlackByte","gdrv")
     and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("create","config","start")
  | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessParentFileName;
let kernelLoad =
  DeviceEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
  | where ActionType in ("DriverLoad","KernelModuleLoad")
  | where FileName in~ (suspiciousDriverFiles)
  | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, FileName, FolderPath, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
driverDrop
| join kind=inner (svcCreate) on DeviceId
| union kernelLoad
| sort by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Generic BYOVD hunts fire on every gamer with MSI Afterburner. This UC specifically chains (a) RTCore64.sys / DBUtil_2_3.sys dropped OUTSIDE the legitimate vendor path, with (b) sc.exe creating a kernel service whose name matches BlackByte's hard-coded service strings ('BlackByte', 'RTCore64'), with (c) an actual DriverLoad event. That triple is exactly what Sophos reverse-engineered out of the Bla

ucs.title: [LLM] BlackByte ExByte staging + MEGA / mega.nz exfiltration prior to ransomware detonation
ucs.description: Hunts BlackByte's custom Go-based exfiltration utility 'ExByte' (a.k.a. ExByte.exe / data.exe), which enumerates files, writes a 'data.txt' inventory, and uploads to mega.nz / mega.io via the MEGA API before the encryptor runs. Behavior pattern is unique to BlackByte affiliates and was first documented by Cisco Talos.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Traffic.dest) as dest values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as port values(All_Traffic.app) as app from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest="*.mega.nz" OR All_Traffic.dest="*.mega.co.nz" OR All_Traffic.dest="*.userstorage.mega.co.nz" OR All_Traffic.dest="g.api.mega.co.nz") by host All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.process_name All_Traffic.user
| join type=inner host [ | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("ExByte.exe","data.exe","explorer.exe","svchost.exe") OR Processes.process="*-single*" OR Processes.process="*data.txt*") by host Processes.process Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.user ]
| append [ | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name IN ("data.txt","l.txt","ExByte.exe") AND Filesystem.file_path="*\\Users\\*" by host Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.process_name ]
| append [ | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process="*vssadmin*delete*shadows*" OR Processes.process="*wmic*shadowcopy*delete*" OR Processes.process="*bcdedit*recoveryenabled*No*") by host Processes.process Processes.user ]
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let megaDomains = dynamic(["mega.nz","mega.co.nz","userstorage.mega.co.nz","g.api.mega.co.nz","mega.io"]);
let exbyteHosts =
  DeviceProcessEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
  | where FileName in~ ("ExByte.exe","data.exe")
     or (ProcessCommandLine has "-single" and ProcessCommandLine has "data.txt")
     or (FileName endswith ".exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "mega.nz")
  | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine, SHA256, InitiatingProcessFileName;
let megaNet =
  DeviceNetworkEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
  | where RemoteUrl has_any (megaDomains) or tostring(parse_url(RemoteUrl).Host) has_any (megaDomains)
  | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","MEGAsync.exe")
  | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessAccountName;
let recoveryKill =
  DeviceProcessEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
  | where (FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("delete","shadows"))
       or (FileName =~ "wmic.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("shadowcopy","delete"))
       or (FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("recoveryenabled","No"))
  | project recoveryTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, recoveryCmd=ProcessCommandLine;
exbyteHosts
| join kind=inner megaNet on DeviceId
| join kind=leftouter recoveryKill on DeviceId
| where datetime_diff('hour', recoveryTime, Timestamp) between (-12 .. 24) or isempty(tostring(recoveryTime))
| sort by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: BlackByte affiliates are the primary public ransomware crew tied to ExByte (Talos, 2022; AA22-321A). The query joins three actor-specific markers in one short window: (1) a binary launching with the literal '-single' flag and writing 'data.txt' staging file in a user profile, (2) non-browser, non-MEGAsync process beaconing to mega.nz/userstorage.mega.co.nz API endpoints, and (3) the BlackByte reco
